items.title: starting here
items.content_text: Blog reset.I’m Vinicius Batista. “apolzek” is a nickname born from the combination of two people I met during the CTF and IRC chat days: Apol-0 and ZekAng3l. With them, I learned far beyond technology. Philosophy, economics, biology, and especially the idea that different and even conflicting perspectives can coexist in the same space.Right now I’m using a picture of Júlio from Cocoricó because he represents exactly what I’m trying to recover: genuine curiosity. Asking more questions, being honest, keeping things simple, and relearning how to learn.This blog is a relatively organized chaos: technical writing, philosophical reflections, occasional confessions, and a few scattered ideas.I mainly write for myself, but I also intend to share texts that may help people growing professionally in areas where I have more experience, such as reliability engineering and platform engineering.This is not just a blog. It is a piece of my original self.Let’s go together. Let’s go with God.
items.content_html:
Blog reset.

I’m Vinicius Batista. “apolzek” is a nickname born from the combination of two people I met during the CTF and IRC chat days: Apol-0 and ZekAng3l. With them, I learned far beyond technology. Philosophy, economics, biology, and especially the idea that different and even conflicting perspectives can coexist in the same space.

Right now I’m using a picture of Júlio from Cocoricó because he represents exactly what I’m trying to recover: genuine curiosity. Asking more questions, being honest, keeping things simple, and relearning how to learn.

This blog is a relatively organized chaos: technical writing, philosophical reflections, occasional confessions, and a few scattered ideas.

I mainly write for myself, but I also intend to share texts that may help people growing professionally in areas where I have more experience, such as reliability engineering and platform engineering.

This is not just a blog. It is a piece of my original self.

Let’s go together. Let’s go with God.
